content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed report:

## Comprehensive Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. Entry Points

**src/index.tsx** - Main entry point
- Creates React root and renders App component
- Uses React 19 with StrictMode
- Service worker is unregistered by default

**src/App.tsx** - Root application component
- Wraps app with three main providers:
  - `Router` (React Router v7)
  - `StoreProvider` (Redux with @reduxjs/toolkit)
  - `ThemeProvider` (styled-components)
- Uses `useDarkMode` hook for theme switching (defaults to light)
- Renders `GlobalStyle` and `AppRoutes`

**src/Routes.tsx** - Route configuration
- Creates modal portal element with id `#modal`
- Routes defined:
  - `/` → HomePage
  - `/categories` → CategoryListPage
  - `/categories/:id` → CategoryDetailPage
  - `/restaurants/:id` → RestaurantDetailPage
  - `/checkout` → CheckoutPage
  - `/success` → SuccessPage
- Implements auto-scroll-to-top on route change

### 2. Providers & Context

**Theme Provider** (styled-components)
- Light and dark themes via `use-dark-mode` hook
- Global state stored in localStorage
- Theme object includes: colors, typography, spacing, borderRadius, boxShadow

**Redux Store** (src/app-state/)
- Store configured with @reduxjs/toolkit v2.6.0
- Two slices: `cart` and `order`
- Custom hooks: `useAppDispatch()` and `useAppSelector()`
- Redux state structure:
  - `cart`: { visible: boolean, items: CartItem[] }
  - `order`: { items: CartItem[] }

**Router Provider**
- react-router-dom v7.2.0
- Layout effect for scroll-to-top on navigation

### 3. Global CSS & Styling

**GlobalStyle.tsx** - Injected globally via styled-components `createGlobalStyle`
- Imports external CSS:
  - `react-multi-carousel/lib/styles.css`
  - `react-loading-skeleton/dist/skeleton.css`
- Defines font faces (Montserrat, Hind) loaded from Google Fonts
- Light-dark mode transitions (250ms ease-in)
- Base CSS reset and utility classes (.container, .footer-bottom, etc.)
- Carousel item styling overrides
- Responsive breakpoints via media queries

**Theme System** (src/styles/theme.ts)
- Base colors: white, black, red, grey, green, blue
- Spacing unit system (xxs to xxl)
- Border radius variants
- Box shadow definitions (card, inner, outerBorder)
- Typography scales
- Two themes: lightTheme and darkTheme

### 4. Data Fetching

**Axios-based API** (src/api/index.ts)
- Base URL: `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants`
- RestaurantsApi class with methods:
  - `getRestaurants()` - Fetches all restaurants
  - `getRestaurantById(id)` - Fetches single restaurant
  - `getRestaurantsByCategory(category)` - Filters by category
- Simple cache implementation (bypassed in test/storybook environments)
- Environment detection: `import.meta.env.STORYBOOK`, `NODE_ENV === 'test'`, `__vitest_browser__`

**Stub Data** (src/stub/)
- restaurants.ts - Full restaurant mock data
- categories.ts - Category definitions
- cart-items.ts - Shopping cart mock data
- Used for component development and testing

### 5. Browser State Management

**localStorage**
- Dark mode preference stored by `use-dark-mode` hook (globalThis.window used)

**sessionStorage**
- Not currently used in codebase

**Cookies**
- Not currently used in codebase

### 6. Portals & Modal Roots

**Portal Component** (src/components/Portal.tsx)
- Custom wrapper around React's `createPortal`
- Accepts selector string for target DOM element
- Uses useRef and useEffect to find element

**Modal Root**
- Element with id `#modal` created in AppRoutes
- Used by Modal component for rendering outside main DOM tree

**Modal Component** (src/components/Modal/Modal.tsx)
- Uses Portal to render to #modal
- CSSTransition animations (300ms)
- Escape key handler via custom `useKey` hook
- Body scroll lock via `useLockBodyScroll` hook
- Click-outside-to-close via backdrop

### 7. Pages & Components Structure

**Pages** (6 main pages):
- HomePage - Displays banner, categories, featured restaurants, award-winning section
- CategoryListPage - Lists all food categories
- CategoryDetailPage - Shows restaurants by category
- RestaurantDetailPage - Full restaurant menu with food items
- CheckoutPage - Multi-step form (delivery + payment)
- SuccessPage - Order confirmation page

**Page Template** (src/templates/PageTemplate.tsx)
- Variants: 'default' (Header + content + Footer), 'sticky-header', 'basic' (logo only)
- Min height constraint on content

**Major Components** (52 component files total):
- **Layout**: Header, Footer, Sidebar, PageSection, Layout
- **UI Components**: Button, Icon, Badge, Breadcrumb, Spinner, TopBanner
- **Cards**: RestaurantCard, FooterCard, Category
- **Forms**: Input, Select (in src/components/forms/)
- **Specialized**: Modal, ShoppingCart, ShoppingCartMenu, Portal, ErrorBlock
- **Typography**: Custom typography components
- **Shopping**: ShoppingCartItem, OrderSummary
- **Media**: AnimatedIllustration (Lottie player), Review
- **Restaurant**: FoodItem, FoodItemModal, FoodSection

### 8. Routing Setup

- Framework: react-router-dom v7.2.0
- Strategy: Client-side routing with pathname-based route matching
- Auto-scroll behavior on navigation
- Modal portal created at root route level
- No route guards or middleware detected
- 6 main routes with dynamic segments (`:id`)

### 9. Package Manager

**Yarn v4.2.1**
- yarn.lock file present
- Configuration: .yarnrc.yml specifies node-modules linker and yarn path
- MSW configured via msw.workerDirectory in package.json

### 10. TypeScript Configuration

**tsconfig.json**:
- Target: ESNext
- Lib: dom, dom.iterable, esnext
- Module: ESNext, bundler resolution
- Strict mode: true
- JSX: react-jsx
- Base URL: src (path aliases from src/)
- Type checking command: `npm run check` (tsc --noEmit)
- Global types: vite/client, vitest/globals, @testing-library/jest-dom

### 11. Storybook Configuration

**.storybook/main.ts**:
- Framework: @storybook/react-vite (using pre-release version)
- Story patterns: ../src/**/*.mdx, ../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx), ./eval-support/*.mdx
- Addons:
  - @chromatic-com/storybook
  - @storybook/addon-vitest (testing integration)
  - @storybook/addon-a11y (accessibility)
  - @storybook/addon-docs
  - @storybook/addon-onboarding

**.storybook/preview.tsx**:
- Basic control matchers for color and date fields
- A11y testing set to 'todo'

### 12. MSW Status

**Installed**: YES (msw ^2.7.3 in devDependencies)

**Configuration**:
- workerDirectory: public
- mockServiceWorker.js present in /public directory
- No explicit handlers/middleware detected in src yet
- Ready for API mocking setup

### 13. Existing Stories

**3 Example Stories** in src/stories/:
- Button.stories.ts - Basic button component showcase (Primary, Secondary, Large, Small)
- Header.stories.ts - Header component (LoggedIn, LoggedOut states)
- Page.stories.ts - Full page template
- Using @storybook/react-vite with fn() for action tracking

**Story Format**:
- TypeScript-based (Meta, StoryObj pattern)
- Uses satisfies operator for type safety
- Includes argTypes and tags: ['autodocs']
- fn() utility from storybook/test for spy tracking

### 14. Testing & Build Setup

**Vitest Configuration** (vitest.config.ts):
- Two project profiles:
  - **node**: happy-dom environment for unit tests (*.test.ts)
  - **storybook**: browser testing with Playwright (chromium)
- Playwright mobile profile option via STORYBOOK_TEST_SCREENSHOTS env var
- @storybook/addon-vitest plugin integration
- Coverage exclusions configured

**Build**:
- Vite with @vitejs/plugin-react
- Output dir: build/
- Dev server port: 3000

**Commands**:
- start: `vite`
- build: `tsc && vite build`
- test: `vitest`
- test:ci: `vitest run`
- storybook: `storybook dev -p 6006`
- check: `tsc --noEmit`

### 15. Code Quality

**ESLint** (eslint.config.js):
- Flat config format with:
  - @typescript-eslint/parser and plugin
  - react plugin (requires detect version, react-in-jsx-scope disabled)
  - unicorn plugin (prevent-abbreviations, null, filename-case disabled)
  - storybook plugin
  - prettier integration

**Prettier**:
- printWidth: 100
- trailingComma: es5
- singleQuote: true
- tabWidth: 2
- semi: false
- bracketSpacing: true

**Lint-staged** (pre-commit hook):
- *.{ts,tsx}: eslint --fix
- *.{html,md}: prettier --write

### 16. Custom Hooks

**useKey** (src/hooks/useKeyboard.ts):
- Tracks keyboard press state
- Accepts key name, keyDown and keyUp callbacks
- Returns boolean for key pressed state

**useLockBodyScroll** (src/hooks/useBodyScrollLock.ts):
- Locks/unlocks document scrolling
- Mobile-specific: also sets height to 100vh
- Cleanup on unmount

### 17. Helper Functions

**src/helpers/**:
- `toCurrency(number)` - Formats numbers as currency (locale-aware)
- `isMobile()` - Detects mobile device via user agent
- `getCurrency()` - Returns currency code (implementation in separate file)

### 18. Form Handling

**Technology**: react-hook-form v7.54.2 with @hookform/resolvers

**Usage**: Checkout page multi-step form
- DeliveryDetails form component
- ContactDetails form component
- StepIndicator for progress
- Validation schema via Zod v3.24.2

### 19. Key Libraries & Dependencies

**Core**:
- React 19, React DOM 19
- React Router DOM 7.2.0
- Redux Toolkit 2.6.0, React-Redux 9.2.0

**Styling**:
- styled-components 6.1.15
- use-dark-mode 2.3.1

**Forms & Validation**:
- react-hook-form 7.54.2
- @hookform/resolvers 4.1.3
- Zod 3.24.2

**UI/Animation**:
- react-transition-group 4.4.5
- react-lottie-player 2.1.0
- react-multi-carousel 2.8.5
- react-loading-skeleton 3.5.0
- react-hooks-helper 1.6.0

**HTTP**:
- axios 1.8.1

**Testing/Storybook**:
- Vitest 4.0.15
- @testing-library/react 16.2.0
- @vitest/browser-playwright 4.0.15
- @storybook/* (pre-release pr-34595 version)
- MSW 2.7.3

**Browser Testing**:
- Playwright 1.50.1
- Happy DOM 17.1.8

### 20. Environment Setup

**Development**:
- Vite v6.2.0
- TypeScript 5.7.3
- Node environment detected via import.meta.env

**Production Build**:
- TypeScript compilation first, then Vite build
- Output to build/ directory
- Service worker optional (currently unregistered)

---

## Summary

This is a **restaurant ordering application** built with React 19, Redux, and styled-components. It features:
- Multi-page app with category browsing, restaurant details, and multi-step checkout
- Light/dark theme switching
- Shopping cart with Redux state management
- Modal-based components using React portals
- Comprehensive TypeScript setup with strict mode
- Vitest + Storybook testing infrastructure
- Axios data fetching with local stub data fallback
- MSW installed but not yet configured with handlers
- Pre-release Storybook with vitest addon integration

The codebase is well-organized with clear separation of concerns, using styled-components for styling, Redux for state, and a clean component architecture with pages, templates, and reusable components.

1→import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'

     3→import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
     4→import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'
     5→
     6→import { store } from './app-state'

    12→  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
    13→  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

    15→  return (
    16→    <Router>
    17→      <StoreProvider store={store}>
    18→        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>

    25→}

1→import { useLayoutEffect } from 'react'

     3→
     4→import { CategoryListPage } from './pages/CategoryListPage'
     5→import { CategoryDetailPage } from './pages/CategoryDetailPage'
     6→import { RestaurantDetailPage } from './pages/RestaurantDetailPage'
     7→import { HomePage } from './pages/HomePage'
     8→import { CheckoutPage } from './pages/CheckoutPage'
     9→import { SuccessPage } from './pages/SuccessPage'
    10→
    11→export const AppRoutes: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<unknown>> = ({ children }) => {
    12→  const location = useLocation()
    13→  // Scroll to top when a path changes
    14→  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    15→    window.scrollTo(0, 0)

    18→  return (

    20→      <div id="modal" />

    23→        <Route path="/categories/:id" element={<CategoryDetailPage />} />
    24→        <Route path="/restaurants/:id" element={<RestaurantDetailPage />} />
    25→        <Route path="/checkout" element={<CheckoutPage />} />

    27→        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}
   101→

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import { CSSTransition } from 'react-transition-group'
     3→
     4→import { Portal } from '../Portal'
     5→import { useKey } from '../../hooks/useKeyboard'
     6→import { Button } from '../Button'
     7→import { useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks/useBodyScrollLock'
     8→
     9→import { ModalContent, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Modal.styles'
    10→
    11→type ModalProps = {
    12→  isOpen: boolean
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    23→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    24→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    25→
    26→  return (
    27→    <Portal selector="#modal">
    28→      <CSSTransition
    29→        in={isOpen}
    30→        timeout={300}
    31→        classNames="modal"
    32→        unmountOnExit
    33→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    34→      >
    35→        <ModalContent data-testid="modal" ref={contentRef}>
    36→          <TopBar>
    37→            <Button
    38→              data-testid="modal-close-btn"
    39→              onClick={onClose}
    40→              clear
    41→              round
    42→              icon="cross"
    43→              aria-label="close modal"
    44→              iconSize={16}
    45→            />
    46→          </TopBar>
    47→          {children}
    48→        </ModalContent>
    49→      </CSSTransition>
    50→
    51→      <CSSTransition
    52→        in={isOpen}
    53→        timeout={300}
    54→        classNames="backdrop"
    55→        unmountOnExit
    56→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    57→      >
    58→        <Backdrop data-testid="modal-backdrop" ref={backdropRef} onClick={onClose} />
    59→      </CSSTransition>

content.text:
Wait — the story decorator will replace the preview decorator but the preview decorator still provides the MemoryRouter. Looking at the error, it seems that Storybook decorators stack, so both the preview decorator (with MemoryRouter) and the story decorator (with MemoryRouter) both run. I need the story-level decorator to completely replace the preview one. 

In Storybook, story decorators run inside preview decorators, so the preview's MemoryRouter still wraps it. The best approach is to not use a story-level MemoryRouter but instead make the preview decorator handle routing better. Actually, the simplest fix: don't use a story decorator, but instead have the preview decorator not nest another router if one already exists. The cleanest solution is to remove the MemoryRouter from the story decorator and instead pass route params via a different approach.

Actually, looking closer: the issue is that my RestaurantDetailPage story decorator wraps in a MemoryRouter, which then gets wrapped by the preview MemoryRouter. I need to skip the preview router for this story. Let me use a different approach - I'll create a wrapper component that uses Routes/Route inside the existing router from preview.

content.text:
Here's a summary of everything that was set up:

## Storybook Setup Complete

### Infrastructure
- **`.storybook/preview.tsx`** — Full app environment with `definePreview`: MemoryRouter, Redux StoreProvider, styled-components ThemeProvider, GlobalStyle, MSW loader
- **`.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`** — MSW handlers for the restaurant API (`getRestaurants`, `getRestaurantById`, `getRestaurantsByCategory`)
- **`.storybook/preview-body.html`** — Portal root `#modal` for the Modal component
- **`.storybook/main.ts`** — Added `staticDirs: ['../public']` for MSW worker
- **`package.json`** — Added `imports` map for `#.storybook/*` and `msw-storybook-addon`
- **`tsconfig.json`** — Added `paths` for `#.storybook/*` resolution

### 10 Story Files (41 tests total)
| File | Stories | Pattern Covered |
|------|---------|----------------|
| `Button/Button.stories.tsx` | 5 (Default, Clear, CssCheck, WithIcon, Disabled) | Low-level reusable component + CSS assertion |
| `Badge/Badge.stories.tsx` | 3 (Default, LongText, Capitalized) | Simple UI component |
| `RestaurantCard/RestaurantCard.stories.tsx` | 4 (Default, New, Closed, Loading) | Card with multiple states |
| `Category/Category.stories.tsx` | 3 (Squared, Rounded, Sushi) | Category display variants |
| `Review/Review.stories.tsx` | 3 (Excellent, VeryGood, NoReviews) | Rating display logic |
| `Header/Header.stories.tsx` | 3 (Default, WithCartItems, LogoOnly) | App header (presentational) |
| `Footer/Footer.stories.tsx` | 3 (Default, WithNavLinks, WithSocialLinks) | App footer |
| `TopBanner/TopBanner.stories.tsx` | 3 (WithTitle, WithPhoto, TitleOnly) | Banner component |
| `RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx` | 3 (Default, WithMenu, Rating) | Page with data fetching via MSW |
| `HomePage/HomePage.stories.tsx` | 3 (Default, WithRestaurants, WithCategories) | Full page with API data |
